feed.title: Combined summary - What if we let Quantum Hunters get Bitcoin rewards ?

entry.title: Combined summary - What if we let Quantum Hunters get Bitcoin rewards ?
entry.summary: The ongoing discussions within the Bitcoin community, particularly concerning quantum computing's threat to cryptocurrency security, have led to innovative proposals such as Amon's draft Informational BIP. This proposal introduces a tripartite mechanism aimed at transforming quantum threats into a self-financing opportunity for advancing Bitcoin's security framework. The first part of this approach involves the Genesis Quantum Transaction (GQT), which validates transactions from quantum-vulnerable addresses and triggers subsequent processes. Following this, the Quantum Vault (QV) collects 90% of these funds, distributing them periodically in line with the Bitcoin halving cycle to support quantum technology advancements. The final element, Quantum Proof Addresses (QPAs), establishes a transparent registry of entities with quantum capabilities while also rewarding these entities with an immediate 10% of transaction funds to encourage participation. Amon’s initiative has sparked a range of responses from the community. Nikita, another developer, expressed concerns over the motivations behind participation in such schemes, questioning the origin of the project and suggesting it might be AI-generated, which raises issues about its authenticity and security. This skepticism points to a broader need for clarity and trust in the project's intentions and mechanisms. Further discourse on the topic includes a contrasting strategy that suggests freezing Bitcoin addresses susceptible to quantum attacks as a safer, albeit less ambitious, alternative. This conservative strategy aims to protect investments from quantum threats until more reliable safeguards are available. Meanwhile, the more dynamic strategy proposed involves incentivizing "hunters" who can demonstrate quantum capability by intercepting vulnerable transactions, thus aligning with Bitcoin’s core principles of fairness and security enhancement. Additionally, there is a discussion around the necessity of integrating quantum resistance into new Bitcoin addresses (BIP-360), requiring existing address holders to transition to these secure addresses. This proactive approach aims to mitigate risks by diversifying potential targets and rewarding those who aid in securing the network against quantum threats. Overall, these discussions highlight the Bitcoin community's active engagement in addressing quantum computing risks through diverse strategies. Each proposal reflects varying degrees of risk, innovation, and alignment with the foundational principles of Bitcoin. As the dialogue continues, it remains crucial for the community to refine these ideas to effectively shield the cryptocurrency from emerging technological disruptions. Amon’s full proposal is open for review and feedback at [this GitHub repository](https://github.com/amonmoce/Hunting-The-Bitcoin-One-Piece/blob/master/bip-hunting-the-bitcoin-one-piece.md).
